title: carpinus caroliniana var. tropicalis
intro: Carpinus caroliniana var. tropicalis, also known as the tropical American hornbeam, is a deciduous tree that is native to the southeastern United States and parts of Central America. Its bark and leaves have been used in traditional medicine for their antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The health benefits of Carpinus caroliniana var. tropicalis include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various ailments. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves are often dried and used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
